text: data/deep's re-opened supply (a-0014) is WIDTH, not DEPTH — and that is exactly what q-0009 needs. Splitting the recursive named-have count over the 1,261 deep decls into top-level frontier WIDTH (the antichain a one-shot section-predictor must emit) vs nesting DEPTH (granularity levels) shows: 633 decls (50.2%) have a >=2-wide top-level named frontier and 384 (30.5%) have >=3 (median 2, max 22) — ample one-shot training signal for a section-predictor, vs only ~27 on corpus_v3. So q-0009 (Variant B) is genuinely unblocked by deeper-module selection. BUT the same split shows nesting depth stays shallow (median 2, max 5; only 5.6% reach depth>=3), so q-0005's binary rough-vs-fine recalibration STANDS even on the deep corpus: there is still no room for a multi-level / U-shape granularity contrast. a-0014 should therefore be read precisely as 'wide-frontier supply'; the concrete next step is Modal round-trip verification of the 633 wide-frontier decls (a now precisely-scoped pipeline spend), not a multi-level redesign.
